### Post by Rafalski on 2006-04-27

### Post by jazzmuzik on 2006-04-27
I was having problems with my ATI card's hi-speed driver, fglrx. There were two things I did and I never get feedback on the first one, so I'm not sure it was even necessary. But do you see that the program has specified **two** video drivers in your code? :

```

Section "Device"
    Identifier "ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon X800 Pro (R420 JI)"
    Driver "ati"
    BusID "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier "ATI Graphics Adapter 0"
    Driver "fglrx"
    BusID "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

```

I completely removed the section with the "ati" driver. Why specify two drivers? That makes no sense.

The second thing I did was add the "KernelModuleParm" "agplock=0" option as you see below. That stopped the screensaver lockups. This is part of my xorg.conf:

```

Section "Device"
        Identifier      "ATI Technologies, Inc. Radeon 9600 (RV350 AS)"
        Driver          "fglrx"
        BusID           "PCI:1:0:0"
        Option          "KernelModuleParm" "agplock=0"
EndSection

```

Good luck. Maybe these suggestions will work for you.

---

### Post by jazzmuzik on 2006-04-28
FWIW, just a minute ago I abandoned using the "fglrx" ATI driver and reverted back to my old xorg.conf file with the "ati" driver. The accelerated driver just has too many problems. I was able to solve some problems, but I ended up getting the vertical bars problem. I'll wait until the manufacturer fixes it.
